article.title: OSHA letter of interpretation clarifies sharps log retention for dental practices
article.summary: An OSHA standard interpretation letter confirms that dental practices must retain sharps injury logs for at least 5 years following the year recorded, consistent with 29 CFR 1904.33. The letter also clarifies that splash exposures count as exposure incidents requiring source-individual testing protocol activation.
article.rawContent: OSHA's Directorate of Enforcement Programs issued a letter of interpretation in response to a query from a state dental association. The letter confirms: (a) sharps injury logs are subject to the 5-year retention requirement under 29 CFR 1904.33; (b) splash exposures of mucous membrane to potentially infectious blood do constitute 'exposure incidents' under 29 CFR 1910.1030(f)(3) and trigger the post-exposure evaluation procedures; (c) employers must offer post-exposure prophylaxis and source-individual HBV/HCV/HIV testing per the standard's appendix.
